title: Galilean Reeh--Schlieder Obstruction
abstract: We prove that the standard Galilean Haag--Kastler axioms, augmented by Bargmann mass superselection, are inconsistent with the Reeh--Schlieder property: no such net admits a vacuum that is cyclic and separating for every local field algebra. Two ingredients combine: Galilean Schrödinger fields annihilate the Fock vacuum, and Bargmann mass superselection forbids the Hermitian-combination evasion that keeps relativistic axioms consistent. The result extends beyond the Fock-representation hypothesis: any Galilean Haag--Kastler net whose canonical fields carry definite Bargmann mass charges and admit time-zero restrictions on a field-algebra-stable common dense domain is incompatible with Reeh--Schlieder. The bounded-below mass spectrum and the vacuum-at-spectral-minimum, usually imposed as separate axioms, are derived consequences -- of positive-energy boost positivity and a Bose-CCR algebraic descent, respectively. The Tomita--Takesaki modular flow is consequently unavailable on Galilean local field algebras. We identify the Reeh--Schlieder property as the precise structural ingredient distinguishing relativistic from Galilean algebraic quantum field theory: relativistic AQFT has it as a theorem, Galilean AQFT cannot.
summary: This paper establishes a fundamental mathematical distinction between relativistic and Galilean quantum field theories. It proves that the Reeh-Schlieder property—which allows for the preparation of local states from the vacuum—cannot exist in a Galilean-invariant theory with mass superselection. The work shows that the absence of this property in Galilean systems leads to the unavailability of Tomita-Takesaki modular flow, marking a deep structural divergence from relativistic physics.
main_problem: The paper investigates whether the Reeh-Schlieder property, a fundamental feature of relativistic algebraic quantum field theory (AQFT), can be maintained within a Galilean-invariant framework.
main_result: The author proves that the standard Galilean Haag-Kastler axioms, when combined with Bargmann mass superselection, are mathematically inconsistent with the Reeh-Schlieder property. This identifies the Reeh-Schlieder property as the defining structural difference between relativistic and Galilean AQFT.
method: The proof utilizes algebraic quantum field theory (AQFT) frameworks, specifically examining the interaction between Galilean Schrödinger fields, Bargmann mass superselection rules, and the properties of the Fock vacuum.
model_or_system: The study focuses on Galilean algebraic quantum field theory (AQFT) nets, specifically those characterized by Schrödinger fields and Bargmann mass superselection charges.
important_parameters: Bargmann mass charges, mass spectrum, and the presence/absence of the Tomita-Takesaki modular flow.
main_assumptions: The proof assumes the standard Galilean Haag-Kastler axioms and the presence of Bargmann mass superselection; it also assumes canonical fields carry definite mass charges and admit time-zero restrictions on a stable common dense domain.
paper_structure: The paper presents a formal proof of inconsistency, demonstrates how the mass spectrum and vacuum properties are derived consequences of the algebraic structure, and concludes by establishing the Reeh-Schlieder property as the boundary between relativistic and Galilean field theories.
